name: 1000 Functional Connectomes Project
description: Multi-site aggregation of resting-state fMRI datasets for mapping intrinsic connectivity

name: ABCD Study (Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development)
description: Longitudinal US cohort on child/adolescent brain development, behavior, and environment

name: AOMIC (Amsterdam Open MRI Collection)
description: Three multimodal MRI datasets (ID1000, PIOP1, PIOP2) for individual-differences research

name: BCP (Baby Connectome Project)
description: Structural and functional MRI of infants/toddlers for early brain connectivity mapping

name: CamCAN (Cambridge Centre for Ageing and Neuroscience)
description: Lifespan cohort with MRI/MEG and cognitive tests for healthy aging

name: dHCP (Developing Human Connectome Project)
description: Neonatal and fetal MRI to characterize early structural and functional connectivity

name: DLBS (Dallas Lifespan Brain Study)
description: Longitudinal multimodal imaging and cognition across the adult lifespan

name: GSP (Brain Genomics Superstruct Project)
description: Young-adult structural and resting fMRI with behavioral and some genetic data

name: HBN (Healthy Brain Network)
description: Pediatric mental health biobank with MRI, EEG, behavior, and genomics

name: HCP – Young Adult (HCP-YA)
description: Flagship young-adult connectome dataset with high-res multi-modal MRI

name: HCP Lifespan – Aging (HCP-A)
description: Lifespan connectome data for midlife to very old adults

name: HCP Lifespan – Development (HCP-D)
description: Structural and functional connectivity in children and adolescents

name: IMAGEN (European Teen Cohort)
description: Longitudinal adolescent imaging-genetics study of mental health and addiction

name: IXI Dataset
description: Multi-scanner structural MRI dataset for normal adult brains

name: LEMON (Leipzig Mind-Brain-Body)
description: Multimodal dataset with MRI, EEG, cognition, emotion, physio in young and old adults

name: NKI-Rockland Sample
description: Community-based lifespan sample with deep phenotyping and multimodal MRI

name: NSPN (NeuroScience in Psychiatry Network)
description: Adolescent and young-adult mental health cohort with MRI and cognitive/clinical measures

name: PING (Pediatric Imaging, Neurocognition, Genetics)
description: Pediatric multi-site normative cohort linking brain, cognition, genetics

name: PNC (Philadelphia Neurodevelopment Cohort)
description: Youth cohort linking clinical risk, cognition, and MRI

name: QTAB (Queensland Twin Adolescent Brain)
description: Twin adolescent neuroimaging cohort, with microbiome and phenotyping

name: QTIM (Queensland Twin Imaging)
description: Young adult twin neuroimaging cohort for heritability of brain structure

name: SALD (Shanghai Adult Lifespan Dataset)
description: Healthy adult lifespan MRI dataset from Chinese cohort

name: SLIM (SWU Longitudinal Imaging Multimodal)
description: Longitudinal test–retest dataset for reliability of structural and functional MRI

name: TAMI Dataset (Taiwan Aging and Mental Illness)
description: MRI cohort for aging and mental illness in Taiwan; used for schizophrenia and aging studies

name: UK Biobank (Imaging)
description: Massive midlife/aging population imaging within UK Biobank

name: VETSA (Vietnam Era Twin Study of Aging)
description: Longitudinal twin study of male aging with MRI and cognition

name: VITA (Vienna Transdanube Aging Study)
description: Population-based 75-year-old cohort in Vienna focusing on dementia prediction

name: 4RTNI (4-Repeat Tauopathy Neuroimaging Initiative)
description: Longitudinal imaging of 4-repeat tauopathies (PSP/CBD) with MRI and tau PET

name: A4 (Anti-Amyloid Treatment in Asymptomatic Alzheimer’s)
description: Clinical trial dataset of amyloid-positive cognitively normal older adults

name: ABIDE (I & II)
description: Multi-site ASD vs control resting-state fMRI and structural MRI

name: ADHD-200
description: Multi-site ADHD vs control resting-state fMRI along with phenotypic data

name: ADNI (Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative)
description: Longitudinal multi-center imaging and biomarker study of Alzheimer’s disease

name: AIBL (Australian Imaging, Biomarkers & Lifestyle)
description: Longitudinal Australian cohort on aging and AD with MRI, PiB-PET, and biomarkers

name: ARWIBO (Italian AD repository)
description: Italian memory clinic cohort with MRI, EEG, and biomarkers for dementia research

name: COBRE (Center for Biomedical Research Excellence)
description: Schizophrenia vs control resting-state fMRI and structural MRI

name: EDSD (European DTI Study on Dementia)
description: Multicenter DTI study on dementia for harmonization and group analyses

name: ENIGMA Consortium
description: Worldwide neuroimaging genetics consortium pooling local analyses for mega/meta-analyses

name: NACC (National Alzheimer’s Coordinating Center)
description: National US clinical dataset with neuropsych, diagnoses, and imaging subsets

name: NIFD (Neuroimaging in Frontotemporal Dementia)
description: Longitudinal MRI and clinical study of FTD and controls

name: OASIS (1, 2, & 3)
description: Open MRI (and PET in OASIS-3) datasets for aging & Alzheimer’s research

name: PPMI (Parkinson’s Progression Markers Initiative)
description: Multimodal biomarker study in early Parkinson’s disease

name: Prevent AD
description: Longitudinal MRI and genetics/CSF study of pre-symptomatic AD risk in older adults

name: REST-meta-MDD
description: Multi-site resting-state fMRI meta-dataset for major depressive disorder

name: SchizConnect
description: Federated portal aggregating MRI datasets for schizophrenia

name: SCZIowa (Iowa Schizophrenia)
description: University of Iowa schizophrenia structural MRI cohort

name: BOLD5000
description: Deep visual fMRI dataset where subjects view ~5k natural images

name: Deep Image Reconstruction (Kamitani)
description: fMRI dataset for reconstructing natural images via deep neural networks

name: Gallant Lab Natural Movie / VIM
description: Natural movie vision fMRI dataset used for encoding/decoding models

name: Generic Object Decoding (Kamitani)
description: fMRI dataset for decoding seen and imagined object categories

name: Haxby “Raiders” Dataset
description: Full-length movie (Raiders of the Lost Ark) fMRI dataset for hyperalignment studies

name: IBC (Individual Brain Charting)
description: High-res multi-task fMRI mapping of a small group of individuals

name: Narratives (Princeton/Hasson Collection)
description: fMRI responses to naturalistic spoken stories with aligned transcripts

name: NSD (Natural Scenes Dataset)
description: Massive 7 T fMRI dataset on natural image viewing for brain–AI comparisons

name: Sherlock (Movie Watching & Recall)
description: fMRI while watching TV episode Sherlock and later free recall

name: StudyForrest (Forrest Gump dataset)
description: Audio & audio-visual movie fMRI dataset, extended with eye-tracking and MEG

name: THINGS-fMRI / THINGS-MEG
description: Large-scale object concepts dataset with thousands of images and corresponding neural data

name: Midnight Scan Club (MSC)
description: Precision individual mapping with 10 dense fMRI sessions per subject

name: MyConnectome
description: Longitudinal single-subject fMRI + multi-omics dataset

name: Allen Mouse Brain Atlas (ABA)
description: Genome-wide in situ hybridization gene expression atlas of the mouse brain

name: Allen Mouse Brain Connectivity Atlas
description: Mesoscale axonal tracer connectivity atlas mapping whole-mouse-brain projections

name: Brain/MINDS Marmoset
description: Marmoset brain MRI, histology, and connectivity datasets

name: C. elegans Connectome
description: Synapse-level wiring diagram of the nematode nervous system

name: FlyEM Drosophila Hemibrain Connectome
description: Large-scale synaptic-resolution EM reconstruction of half a Drosophila brain

name: IBL Brain-Wide Map
description: Open mouse brain-wide Neuropixels recordings from the International Brain Laboratory during a visual decision-making task.
modalities_notes: Includes brain-wide extracellular Neuropixels recordings with trial structure, wheel movement, video/pupil-derived state signals, histology/alignment metadata, Allen CCF registration, and access through the IBL ONE/ALF toolchain.

name: MICrONS (Machine Intelligence from Cortical Networks)
description: Mouse visual cortex resource spanning electron microscopy connectomics and two-photon calcium imaging
modalities_notes: Access entry points: DANDI functional NWB data https://dandiarchive.org/dandiset/000402/draft; BossDB/static repositories https://tutorial.microns-explorer.org/static-repositories.html; CAVE programmatic access https://tutorial.microns-explorer.org/quickstart_notebooks/01-caveclient-setup.html.

name: PRIME-DE (PRIMate Data Exchange)
description: Global resource of non-human primate MRI data

name: PRIME-RE (PRIMatE Resource Exchange)
description: Portal for broader NHP resources (MRI, PET, protocols, code)

name: Allen BrainSpan (Developing Human Brain)
description: Transcriptomic and epigenomic atlas of developing human brain

name: Allen Cell Types Database
description: Multimodal characterization of individual neurons (ephys, morphology, transcriptomics)

name: Allen Human Brain Atlas (AHBA)
description: Genome-wide microarray expression atlas of adult human brain

name: BigBrain Atlas
description: 20 µm 3D histological reconstruction of a human brain

name: BrainMap
description: Curated database of stereotactic coordinates from functional neuroimaging studies

name: CoRR (Consortium for Reliability & Reproducibility)
description: Collection of test–retest MRI datasets for reliability assessment

name: CRCNS (Collaborative Research in Computational Neuroscience)
description: Repository of electrophysiology and some imaging data for model development

name: DANDI Archive
description: BRAIN Initiative archive for neurophysiology & imaging data in NWB format

name: Figshare (general data repository)
description: General-purpose repository hosting many independent neuro datasets

name: H01 Human Cortical Connectome
description: Synaptic-resolution EM volume of human temporal cortex (~1 mm³)

name: NeuroQuery
description: Text-to-brain map meta-analytic model built from large fMRI literature

name: NeuroSynth
description: Automated meta-analysis platform linking terms to brain activation

name: NeuroVault
description: Open repository of unthresholded statistical maps, atlases & ROIs

name: Open Science Framework (OSF)
description: General open-science platform hosting many behavioral/neuro datasets

name: PsychENCODE
description: Large multi-omic datasets from human postmortem brains for psychiatric genomics

name: All of Us (Precision Medicine Cohort)
description: US national cohort linking EHR, biosamples, genomics; some imaging subcohorts
center: NIH / All of Us Research Program
principal_investigator: NIH / All of Us Research Program

name: BBC LabUK Cognitive Test Dataset
description: Large online cognitive dataset collected via BBC Lab UK platform

name: IEEG Portal
description: Portal for sharing clinical intracranial EEG and scalp EEG recordings

name: LYRIKS / PRESCIENT
description: ECoG / iEEG datasets around language and memory (scientific projects)

name: ManyLabs / ManyBabies
description: Multi-lab behavioral replication projects pooling data across laboratories

name: MIMIC (III & IV)
description: De-identified ICU EHR (and MIMIC-IV-EEG, waveform subsets)

name: N3C (National COVID Cohort Collaborative)
description: US-wide harmonized COVID-19 EHR dataset

name: Neurodata Without Borders (NWB)
description: Data standard and ecosystem for neurophysiology; many datasets share NWB format
size_human: standard; format; not a single dataset

name: OMEGA (Open MEG Archive)
description: Repository of resting and task MEG datasets with structural MRI

name: OpenPsychometrics
description: Collection of psychological tests and survey datasets

name: RAM (Restoring Active Memory)
description: ECoG/iEEG memory experiments in epilepsy patients

name: TestMyBrain.org
description: Web-based experimental platform with large-scale cognitive test datasets

name: TUH EEG Corpus
description: Large clinical EEG corpus from Temple University Hospital

name: Blue Brain Project
description: Detailed digital reconstruction and simulation of neocortical microcircuits

name: BrainWeb (Digital Brain Phantom)
description: MRI phantoms to simulate realistic structural brain MR images

name: NetPyNE Model Data
description: Example network models and simulation outputs for spiking neural networks

name: TVB (The Virtual Brain)
description: Platform and example datasets for whole-brain network simulations
